programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. Meanwhile, Leo faces a difficult choice when he is asked to prove his pathologist mentor made a mistake in a case that resulted in a mother being imprisoned for smothering her baby to death. Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves star

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The news of his grandmother's death sends Pascoe into mourning, and while sorting her belongings, he discovers his grandfather may have been a First World War deserter. Unable to accept this, he resolves to uncover the truth. Meanwhile, Wield and Dalziel investigate a raid by animal rights activists on a pharmaceutical company. Starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan, with Frances Barber, Shaun Dooley and Jimmy Reddington

programme.desc: The team re-examines the murder of a junior doctor strangled in 2010. Depressed at the thought of his daughter Holly leaving home for university, Danny finds himself being cheered by his colleagues as they hire a pedibus for a ride through central London. Tamzin Outhwaite and Nicholas Lyndhurst star

programme.desc: A pub landlady is stabbed to death and has the tip of her little finger cut off, and while Leo and Jack collect mounting evidence that her husband was responsible, Nikki remains convinced of the man's innocence. When he is charged with the crime, she must convince her colleagues the woman was the latest victim of a psychopathic serial killer, resulting in a deadly game of cat and mouse as they seek out the culprit. programme.desc: The final episode of the popular British sitcom about the cheeky painter. Jacko's world is falling apart - or is it?

The team's respect for Sam is shaken when his 21st-century investigation methods put them at risk, but he insists the link is too obvious and tries to open his boss's eyes to the benefits of good community relations

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team looks into the death of a terrorist 30 years previously after his daughter receives an anonymous note claiming he was murdered - an investigation that takes them right back to the Greenham Common anti-nuclear protests. Meanwhile, Sasha agrees to go for dinner with her ex-husband Ned, which he is hoping will lead to a reconciliation. Tamzin Outhwaite and Dennis Waterman star, with guest appearances from Barnaby Kay, Charlotte Cornwell and Patricia Potter

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of a teenager's remains halts an eco-housing project, putting Nikki and Jack under pressure to help solve the mystery so construction can continue - and leading to an unexpected romance for Nikki. Meanwhile, Leo is caught up in a dangerous hospital siege when distraught father Tom Hancock holds a ward at gunpoint, insisting his terminally ill son's life-support is switched off. Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves star, with Mark Womack and Ed Stoppard

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sally and Will follow up a report from a shocked driver who accidentally hit a pedestrian with her car, only to see the victim run off. The trail leads to teenage runaway Adam Younger, who is bleeding from a shotgun wound after befriending homeless Jimmy Chadwick and getting involved with a professional hitman. A sting operation is called off when the gun-man turns up looking for the teenager

programme.desc: Cameras follow country folk pop performer Andrew Walton, aka the Welsh Whisperer, on his journey through rural villages of Wales, meeting characters who are integral to their local communities and village life

programme.title: Ni Yw'r Ffermwyr Ifanc
programme.desc: A chance to get to know the Young Farmers through the eyes of four members and former members, and through items about members across Wales

programme.title: Bregus
programme.desc: Successful career woman Ellie seems to have it all - the perfect husband, daughter and close-knit group of friends. Her unbreakable gang are all in their mid-thirties and depend on each other - they're a family. However, tragedy strikes and unleashes demons in Ellie that prompt her to go on the run, threatening the facade of her life and even the core of her sanity. Psychological drama, starring Hannah Daniel

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Sally Nugent and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in the Devon town of Seaton, Crewe in Cheshire and London's South Norwood

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Pete and Gary are called to a patient in Paisley who has fallen down the stairs, and quickly realise he needs more support than just medical care. Martyn and Kevin treat an elderly patient who has fallen on an icy pedestrianised street

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley offer expert advice at Southwell racecourse, Nottinghamshire, as two good friends take on a father and son-in-law. Anita Manning hosts

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid receives a challenge from an old friend and Valerie gets a mysterious letter. Luca deals with a family whose need for travel vaccinations leads to trouble

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits the Peak District with a couple returning from the Netherlands, who want their generous £1.5million budget to provide accommodation for their visiting mothers

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband and wife Raymond and Karen are both vegan but are bored of making the same old thing. Paired with chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, they hope to come away with some new dishes for their repertoire. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Electrics expert Mark Stuckey and silversmith Brenton West join forces to repair a much-loved, sparkling chandelier that once took pride of place in a council house. Furniture restorer Will Kirk is presented with a damaged mother of pearl table, badly burned after a hot pan was mistakenly placed on its surface. And mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a broken toy aeroplane that doubles up as a memento of a lost childhood in Iraq

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating mark the start of the first lockdown with an hour-long special looking at some of the more positive stories and achievements of the past 12 months, and celebrating how people across the country have stepped up to help others

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Metal expert Dominic Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk join forces on a wooden travelling trunk, which was used by a family of Polish refugees as they travelled across continents looking for a new home after the Second World War. Steve Fletcher has trouble getting a wobbly toy cyclist back on the straight and narrow, Brenton West tries to make an old bell ring again and Kirsten Ramsay is asked to repair a damaged glass trophy, which a couple want to pass on in memory of their late daughter, the original recipient

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts this property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house. It's then up to celebrity judges Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix - joined each week by a special guest - to watch the four 'owners' interact and figure out which one is telling the truth and who are the impostors. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen joins in the fun for the first edition, set in a converted barn in Ashford, Kent

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The documentary moves to Newcastle with the stories of survivors who suffered at the hands of George Ormond, a youth coach connected to Newcastle United - revealing how one former player tried to secretly record a confession from his abuser. Eventually, the three separate cases are brought to trial, but the question remains - why did it take so long to reach this point?

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Caitlin is now pregnant with her boss Kate's baby, and they navigate the complex relationship between a surrogate and intended parent, while also working together every day. Gay couple Alex and Richard begin the awkward process of home inseminations with their surrogate, Maddie, while single man David and surrogate Lauren end their relationship

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on a three-and-a-half year project whose auction lot included a fish and chip shop, a flat and a large storage area in Fareham in Hampshire. Dion Dublin checks out a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands, while Lucy Alexander takes a look at a studio flat in London's South Norwood. Two of the three projects prove really challenging, with some unforeseen problems to overcome

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley are live, asking for viewers' help with unsolved cases around the country

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In a Lanarkshire forest, a quad biking accident tests the ambulance service's specialist off-road vehicle to its limits, as Paramedic John struggles to reach the injured patient. In Glasgow, John and Suzanne rush to the scene of a nasty bicycle accident, and in the Borders Lee and Nadine try and ease the pain of a patient with a suspected dislocated shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear meets the infamous Miranda Evans and Sid puzzles over his first labour. Emma has to get a fractured family to come together when a graduation day becomes too much

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Shropshire countryside to help a couple with a £335,000 budget find a rural family home closer to loved ones. Exploring more of the county's bricks and mortar, Jonnie also visits the recently restored 18th-century walled garden at Apley Estate, where he samples freshly grown culinary delights

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Father-and-son Mark and Henry are passionate about good pork, having a smallholding where they rear a heritage pig breed in the Wirral. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as the pair bring some some friendly rivalry to the kitchen, aided by chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher and woodwork expert Will Kirk cannot resist staging a competitive game of pinball on a penny slot machine in need of rejuvenation. Other items seeking attention are a 1960s hand-painted drag racing cone and a pair of art deco-style silver ladies holding mirrors. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray gets an unexpected call from school regarding an issue with one of the children, and when Whitney offers support, he uses the opportunity to plant more seeds of doubt about Kush. Ruby tells Jean she will ring the police and drop the charges against Stacey, but soon she has more to worry about after bad news from the doctor. Ben helps Kheerat teach the thief a lesson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat gives Vinny an opportunity to prove himself by looking after Stas's associate - but he is disheartened when he learns he is to be his driver. Kush plans a romantic evening but Whitney has to work at Gray's, so he brings the date night to her. But when Gray returns home, he is furious to see what is happening

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last heat sees the final five amateurs cook their Signature Dish - something that tells judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace about their potential, as they pick their two favourites with those cooks earning a place in the next round. The remaining three have to pull out all the stops as they cook-off in a new test - the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. After which, those remaining must cook two courses that excite not just John and Gregg but also to former champions Thomasina Miers and Tim Anderson, as they vie for a quarter-final place and the chance to be MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last quarter-final sees the week's quartet of talented heat-winners return to fight for a semi-final place by facing the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by celebrated food critic Grace Dent - is to cook a dish inspired by global street food. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Medical journalist Michael Mosley and a team of scientists investigate some of the easiest and most effective ways to get the biggest benefits of exercise, and turning what we think we know about fitness on its head. The team reveal why walking 10,000 steps a day is just a marketing ploy, and that two minutes of intense exercise is all you need per week. Elsewhere in the programme, they also discuss ways to help you stick to your fitness plan, and discuss exercises that can actually have a positive impact on brain activity

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Wimbledon to meet a pair of young developers with big plans for a two-bed house. He also visits a three-bed semi in Birmingham. Dion Dublin is in Bootle, Merseyside, to cast his eye over a two-bed semi-detached house

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases around the country

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics respond to a major incident on the A90 near Dundee. A crash between a van and a bus has led to multiple casualties, including one patient thrown from the top deck of the bus. Team Leader Mark must prioritise who needs the most urgent care as he works with dozens of other emergency responders at the scene of the crash

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction. Natasha also finds out about the value of provenance

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Here, artists travel to the Wirral to create artworks to complement a recently completed house renovation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple on their country house hunt in Wiltshire. They have a generous £1million budget to afford them space for their prized possessions - a baby grand piano and classic cars. Away from the property search, Nicki discovers more about the art of wooden hat block making, a critically endangered heritage craft being kept alive by a Wiltshire-based family whose blocks are used by milliners across the world

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts, as London-based sisters Kavita and Sangeeta compete. The siblings both have busy lives and would love to get some new ideas for quick tasty meals they can make when they get home after a long day. They are aided in the kitchen by chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Art conservator Lucia Scalisi revives an oil painting of a mining scene, created by an amateur artist in the 1960s. Embroidery expert Sara Dennis and silversmith Brenton West join forces on a delicate wedding tiara seeking attention, while Steve Fletcher takes receipt of an early 20th-century chiming mantlepiece clock that was gifted to a couple on their wedding day. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Liam Neeson and Micheal Richardson, who are playing father and son in comedy-drama Made in Italy. Breakout star of The Serpent, Tahar Rahim, talks about his gripping new legal drama The Mauritanian, while Cush Jumbo reflects on five-part thriller The Beast Must Die, in which he stars. Comedian and actor Rob Brydon and the undisputed queen of Dragons' Den Deborah Meaden also feature. With music by singer-songwriter Laura Mvula

programme.title: La La Land (2016)
programme.desc: Jobbing actress Mia and struggling jazz pianist Sebastian fall in love and attempt to realise their respective dreams in Los Angeles - a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. However, keeping the wolf from the door without compromising their beliefs proves harder than expected. Damien Chazelle's Bafta-winning musical, starring Emma Stone, Ryan Gosling and John Legend

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Best friends Sandra and Rachel have recently embarked on a weight loss and healthier lifestyle plan, and are hoping to get some new ideas to bring fantastic flavour to ingredients they know well. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as they bring some some friendly rivalry to the kitchen, with help from chefs Romy Gill and Anna Haugh

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As they fight to secure a place in the semi-finals, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two hair salons in Richmond, London, and for the first time in the competition, the designers will see their spaces before creating their visions. Presenter Alan Carr and judge Michelle Ogundehin will then decide who makes it through to the next stage

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Gethin Jones presents Wellbeing Wednesday, alongside science and French

programme.title: Earth: The Power of the Planet
programme.desc: Dr Iain Stewart examines the role of ice in changing the course of human evolution and shaping the planet's landscape, visiting a frozen waterfall, a unique lab in Arctic Norway and the granite peaks of Yosemite to learn more about the phenomenal power of glaciers. He also travels to Greenland to examine the evidence of climate change, and asks what the consequences will be if Earth's ice continues to melt

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: David Harper takes on Philip Serrell at a car boot sale in Chesterfield, where David finds an unusual antique record player, while Philip takes a trip to the Dean Forest Railway

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes the annual melting of more than three million square miles of Arctic sea ice, which can be perilous for indigenous species. He also assesses fears that the region could be ice-free throughout the summer months in 50 years' time, and speculates on the results for native polar bears, whales and guillemot chicks

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores how the brain of the great ape has evolved, revealing that different areas have been adapted over time by the animal's anatomy, ingenuity and sociability. He also examines how the ability to use two hands asymmetrically sets the great apes apart from other tool-using animals, and how social living is linked to the evolution of the amygdala in both apes and humans

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charles Hanson and Adam Partridge unearth all manner of items to auction in Morecambe, Lancashire, including French porcelain and Japanese carvings. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the railway station that acted as the set for the iconic film Brief Encounter

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Pizza chefs Jez and Ieuz continue their adventure across Europe, where they cook pizzas for locals in Bardi, Italy, before meeting one of their heroes in Rome

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit a biker bar in Missouri to find out about the origins of Route 66, before heading west, where they visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to the road. They also stop off for lunch in Kansas, sample Yorkshire pudding wraps in Tulsa and finally reach rural Oklahoma, where they cook with members of the Cherokee nation

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition returns for another series, four chefs are welcomed to the kitchen where they prepare canapes, starters and fish courses, inspired by this year's theme which celebrates British invention and innovation. With one of the four being sent home, the pressure is on to produce something delicious and ingenious

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: With the end of their provisions in sight, the officers contemplate a tough, risky strategy. To boost the men's morale, Fitzjames organises a carnival on the ice. Goodsir begins feeding Sir John's pet Capuchin monkey in order to prove that the canned food is causing lead poisoning

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: Captain Crozier gives the order to abandon both ships, leaving a small group of men behind with enough provisions to last the winter, in the event a thaw frees the vessels. A series of flashbacks to 1845 tell the story of the voyage's beginnings through the eyes of an ordinary sailor

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton heads for Tiree, which boasts one of the best sunshine records in the UK

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Secret Science of Sewage
programme.desc: Dr George McGavin, Dr Zoe Laughlin and a team of world-leading scientists explore the biology, chemistry, natural history and social history of faeces from a TV-base camp like no other - within the pools, tanks and bowels of one of the UK's largest and most technologically advanced sewage works

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The presenter treks through the southern fjords of Norway to find the stunning Oseberg ship. This lost 1,000-year-old vessel transformed the fierce reputation of the Vikings and became a symbol of a nation's fight for freedom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants cook their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two participants, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. Gifted with their chosen favourite ingredient, as well as a larder, they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish that showcases it. Arbitrators in this edition include 2015 series champion Simon Wood and finalist Tony Rodd

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to the Black Mountains in south-east Wales to help Alex and Laura finish building a tiny house on wheels

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband and wife Raymond and Karen are both vegan but are bored of making the same old thing. Paired with chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, they hope to come away with some new dishes for their repertoire. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces a look at animals with impressive communication skills, discovering how white-throated sparrows use song to attract mates and the complex vocabulary of prairie dogs. The programme reveals how dolphins are able to address each other by name and chimpanzees use sign language, and also reveals species that communicate using ultrasound, vibrations and parts of the light spectrum beyond the ability of human sense to process

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Thinking Thursday with Cel Spellman

programme.title: Earth: The Power of the Planet
programme.desc: Dr Iain Stewart examines the role of oceans in the formation of Earth as it is today. From the shaping of coastlines to the phenomenon of El Nino, the energy of the current is the most consistent in nature and when it fails the effects are catastrophic. Investigating what will happen if global warming proceeds as predicted, the presenter discovers carbon dioxide will acidify and destroy many nutrients. When last that happened, 250 million years ago, it led to the biggest mass extinction in history

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: David Harper and Phil Serrell go head to head at auction in Royal Leamington Spa, where Phil accidentally buys a lot and David bids on a piece of American Civil War weaponry

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes the annual return of half a billion Pacific salmon to the rivers of Canada and Alaska where they were born - and where bears hungrily await them. Every year, the fish travel up to 20,000 miles to spawn and die, swimming upstream and leaping over giant waterfalls as part of an extraordinary natural event that helps sustain the entire ecosystem

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines what lies beneath a crocodile's hard exterior, discovering the secret to its ancient history and gaining an insight into one of the most sophisticated physiologies on the planet. By following all stages of a crocodile hunt, Chris reveals how their extraordinary circulatory system enables them to hold their breath for more than an hour, and looks at how their exceptionally sensitive skin detects their prey through water

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by antiques experts James Lewis and Caroline Hawley at Wolverhampton Art Gallery, which boasts an extensive collection of paintings. Turning up out of the hundreds of bags and boxes is a sword with a dark history and an early Moorcroft vase. Host Paul also explores some of the region's industrial past

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding test their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Jez and Ieuz's trip leads them to Naples, the spiritual home of pizza. It's a matter of days before the competition, so they need to focus on tracking down the best ingredients for their pizza. They're also keen to pick up any last minute tips, so they arrange a series of masterclasses with some of the most renowned pizzaiolos in the city

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: The third leg of Dave Myers and Si King's trip sees them on the trail of Oklahoma's famous beef. In Oklahoma City, a classic T-bone steak is followed by a visit to the city's Vietnamese community, where they knock up their national dish pho - a broth made of beef or chicken. After checking out El Reno's unique onion burger the duo hook up with a couple of fire crews who like to cook - especially brisket. An organic sweet potato farm inspires Dave and Si to make sweet potato brownies, before they finish this stretch on a cattle ranch with America's most iconic custodian of the land - the cowboy

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Among the mains are a venison dish in honour of the only British woman to win a Nobel prize in science, and a homage to West Midlands manufacturing. The desserts include two layered chocolate dishes - one using edible paper as a tribute to Samuel Johnson, the other featuring an ingenious chocolate cross-section of a road inspired by the invention of smooth tarmac. But whose inventions in the kitchen will veteran judge Lisa Goodwin-Allen prefer? Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Black Power: A British Story of Resistance
programme.desc: A look at the 1960s and '70s through the eyes of the young black people who refused to take racism as a part of the natural order of things in the UK. The documentary shows how the civil rights struggle in America inspired activists to take a stand, and features rare archive of Martin Luther King, Malcolm X and Stokely Carmichael's activities in Britain, as well as footage of leading figures in the movement in the UK including Altheia Jones-LeCointe, Darcus Howe and Roy Sawh

programme.title: Soon Gone: A Windrush Chronicle
programme.desc: A teenage mechanic who left school at the first opportunity finds himself the only black employee in a London garage in 1968. He wrestles with the demands of a changing world and his place in his own family, trying to respect his parents while envying the attention paid to his half-sister. Monologue by Clint Dyer, performed by Jonathan Jules

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: Police are called after a wealthy man suspected to be linked to drug crime was taken from his home in central Manchester by an armed gang. After locating the address where the victim was held and tortured, forensic evidence leads officers to name suspects, but while the hunt is on to find these dangerous criminals, another horrific kidnap happens

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, Stacy meets 31-year-old Duri, who fears she may carry a potentially fatal genetic mutation and wants to test her DNA to see if she could suffer from the same disease as her mother. Elsewhere, a woman who was adopted when she was just six-months-old wants to use DNA technology to finally uncover information about her ancestry

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by William Sitwell, one of the UK's toughest food critics - is to cook a dish inspired by a holiday. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and the team meet semi-retired couple Becky and David to help them convert their drab old yurt into an a colourful and inviting bijou B&B

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Father-and-son Mark and Henry are passionate about good pork, having a smallholding where they rear a heritage pig breed in the Wirral. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as the pair bring some some friendly rivalry to the kitchen, aided by chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Foundation favourites, including History, D&T, Geography, French and Spanish

programme.title: Earth: The Power of the Planet
programme.desc: Dr Iain Stewart looks at Earth's place in time and space. By examining the destruction of species and the fall of empires, he demonstrates the strength of the planet in comparison with human vulnerability. The biggest factors in this protection are Earth's unique position in the solar system, protected by the gravitational pull of the larger Jupiter, with the moon providing stability. By studying Mars, he shows just how lucky mankind has been

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: This week's bargain-hunting competition continues at Newark Antiques Fair, where Philip Serrell uncovers an item used by a secret society and David Harper buys a Cold War motorcycle. Will he ride off with the most profit?

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and David Harper near the end of their week-long battle, competing over four different locations to find the best antiques to turn a profit on. It's a tense affair as they approach the showdown auction

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At St Albans Cathedral and Abbey, Hertfordshire, Claire Rawle discovers an arts and crafts bowl that may have belonged to George Bernard Shaw's wife, and Jonathan Pratt finds a drawing by the artist Laura Knight. Presenter Paul Martin heads underground as he explores Royston Cave, which boasts medieval carvings

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Jez and Ieuz have one time for one last masterclass in Napoli before leaving for Parma. There they pick up a final sprinkling of parmesan, mix the most important batch of dough they will ever make and try to get a good night's sleep before the competition. Then destiny awaits as they face the best pizzaiolos on the planet at the Pizza World Championship

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Edwina Currie, Fatima Whitbread, Mim Shaikh, Amar Latif, Dom Joly and Pauline McLynn spend their last day in Serbia at a monastery hidden in the forests of the Sicevo gorge. Before leaving, Adrian leads the group in a ritual of remembrance with each pilgrim leaving a candle for a loved one. The group travels on to Bulgarian capital Sofia, where Mim and Amar explore its only mosque and Dom, Edwina and Fatima visit the museum of socialist art, a memorial to the nation's communist past. The group reunites and follows the trail towards the Rila Mountains, the highest in the Balkans

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the central region go head to head and cook their six-course menus for panel of judges and Britain's first astronaut, Helen Sharman. How will she judge dishes celebrating British science and innovation, including two dishes paying homage to Professor Stephen Hawking's theory of black holes?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes the most of the clocks going forward as he begins to sow dahlia seeds for summer flowers. He also reveals a new project, and shows what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Adam Frost extends the planting in his gravel garden and reveals his plans for the coming year

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The pianist chats to singer and actor Olly Alexander, recently seen in It's a Sin. Rising star Griff makes her British TV debut, and performs her dark pop anthem Black Hole. Plus, composer and musical polymath Nitin Sawhney and the cellist and singer Ayanna Witter-Johnson collaborate on a duet called Movement II

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Faisal Islam

programme.title: Detroit (2017)
programme.desc: A group of rogue police officers responds to a complaint with apparent retribution rather than justice in mind, launching a raid on a group of African-Americans. Public anger reaches a peak after the incident, as relationships sour with authority. Fact-based drama set during the 1967 Detroit riots, starring John Boyega, Anthony Mackie and Algee Smith

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. The start of Baltar's trial threatens to create divisions within the fleet, and some witnesses believe he may have divine powers. Meanwhile, Lee assists Romo Lampkin in the defence of the imprisoned ex-president

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Baltar's trial reaches its conclusion, key members of the fleet find out they may be the final five Cylons. Meanwhile, Tigh tries to work out why he is hearing music in his head

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the first UK lockdown, Jane Corbin investigates Covid-19 policies pursued by governments around the world, and sets out to discover which nations have had the most successful approach, how the outcomes can be analysed, and attempts to understand what lessons might be learned for future pandemics

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam in which a woman ended up £20,000 out of pocket after beginning an online relationship with a US Navy captain

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that emphasises a slow river winding through colourful foliage, with the final embers of sunlight ahead

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Royal Palace Secrets
programme.desc: The historian goes behind closed doors at three treasure-filled royal palaces to uncover how each building has shaped a monarchy and a nation. With exclusive access to the palaces' most secret places, she uncovers the dramatic events that changed the course of British history, from might and intimidation at the Tower of London and the rise and fall of absolute monarchy at Hampton Court, to the modern face of royalty at Kensington Palace

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: With the rebellion reaching boiling point in Ireland, Elizabeth sends the dashing Earl of Essex to help quell the crisis. Glenda Jackson and Robin Ellis star

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Elizabeth is dead and James VI of Scotland travels south to take the throne at the invitation of Robert Cecil, but the new king has his own ideas and has little time for the late queen's spymaster. Soon Cecil's problems worsen when learns that his arch enemy John Gerard has escaped detention and made contact with a splinter cell in the Catholic underground with a plan to blow-up Parliament. Cecil must negotiate between warring factions to bring down what would become known as the Gunpowder Plot

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: John Freeman quizzes Oscar-winning French actress Simone Signoret, famous for her roles in La Ronde, Les Diaboliques and Room at the Top

programme.title: France 1939: One Last Summer
programme.desc: A collection of home movies, diaries and letters from the last summer before the start of the Second World War reveal how far the impending conflict was away from the minds of ordinary French people. The film describes the final weeks of peace in France and the mix of blindness, denial and prophetic clear-sightedness of those facing the war that was about to unfold

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: A chronicle of New York's drag scene in the 1980s, focusing on balls, voguing and the ambitions and dreams of those who gave the era its warmth and vitality

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that emphasises a slow river winding through colourful foliage, with the final embers of sunlight ahead

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Royal Palace Secrets
programme.desc: The historian goes behind closed doors at three treasure-filled royal palaces to uncover how each building has shaped a monarchy and a nation. With exclusive access to the palaces' most secret places, she uncovers the dramatic events that changed the course of British history, from might and intimidation at the Tower of London and the rise and fall of absolute monarchy at Hampton Court, to the modern face of royalty at Kensington Palace

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: The Atlantic puffins congregating on Skomer Island to breed. Over the course of a summer, cameras follow their adventures on this enchanting location

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a relaxing summer mountain scene, capturing the serene backdrop of warm water and luxurious green grass

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Dalbys' long spell of misfortune comes to an end as James stumbles on the cause of the disease afflicting the family's herd. Mrs Hall advises James to get on with it vis-a-vis Helen and warns him that he risks losing her, with Richard Edmundson still vying for her affections. James finally pops the question, but shudders at the thought of having to ask Mr Alderson's permission

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Western, starring Armie Hammer, Johnny Depp and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Glen Campbell: The Rhinestone Cowboy
programme.desc: An insight into the life of country music star Glen Campbell, documenting his remarkable journey from an impoverished childhood to worldwide fame and success as a guitarist and singer with records including Wichita Lineman and Rhinestone Cowboy. Featuring contributions by family, friends and colleagues including Jimmy Webb, Micky Dolenz and Bob Harris

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank tries to find the Ark of the Covenant, travelling across Jordan, Israel, Palestine and Ethiopia in search of the lost relic. Along the way he visits the ancient city of Petra, scales a sheer cliff face to reach a remote monastery, learns the legends of the most powerful king in Africa, and encounters the Guardian Monk - who allows none to pass

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a relaxing summer mountain scene, capturing the serene backdrop of warm water and luxurious green grass

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls

programme.title: Gravity and Me: The Force That Shapes Our Lives
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili investigates the amazing science of gravity, recreating groundbreaking experiments, including the moment when Galileo first worked out how to measure it. He investigates gravitational waves, finds out from astronauts what it's like to live without gravity, sets out to find where in Britain gravity is weakest and so where we weigh the least, and helps design a smartphone app that volunteers use to demonstrate how gravity affects time and makes us age at slightly different rates

programme.title: Elton John: Electric Prom
programme.desc: The singer is joined by friend and mentor Leon Russell to perform songs from their collaborative album The Union, along with a selection of their most famous hits, at the Roundhouse in London. The duo are also joined on stage by guest singers Plan B and Rumer

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on November 1, with performances by artists including Kim Appleby, Black Box, Roxette, Kylie Minogue, Robert Palmer and UB40, Whitney Houston, Rita MacNeil, the Righteous Brothers and George Michael

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on November 8, with performances by artists including Jason Donovan, EMF, the La's, Jimmy Somerville, 808 State, Paul Simon, Del Amitri, Gazza & Lindisfarne and the Righteous Brothers

programme.title: Elton John at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of interviews, news clips and archive BBC session performances from throughout the singer's career, including footage from The Old Grey Whistle Test and Top of the Pops. Songs featured include Saturday Night's Alright (For Fighting), I'm Still Standing and Circle of Life

programme.title: Elton John: Uncensored
programme.desc: A 2019 programme in which the musician chats to Graham Norton about his eventful life and a career that spans more than 50 years. Elton discusses his childhood growing up as Reg Dwight in post-war Pinner, Middlesex, and looks back at his rise to global stardom in the 1970s. He also shares musical memories of the 80s and '90s, discusses his addictions, and how he managed to overcome them to become one of the world's most successful and prolific solo artists

programme.title: Michael Kiwanuka and Laura Marling at 6 Music Festival
programme.desc: Cerys Matthews presents highlights from the BBC 6 Music Festival 2021 at Alexandra Palace. Songs include You Ain't the Problem by Michael Kiwanuka, Song for Our Daughter by Laura Marling and a special track from Bicep. Cerys also talks to Michael and Laura about being able to finally perform in a live venue after a year of restrictions, albeit with no audience just yet

programme.title: Glam Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the outburst of spangly pop and fuzz-filled rock that lit up the British charts in the early 1970s. Top of the Pops provided the genre's primary TV arena, adding some gloriously gaudy visual effects to the singers' already outrageous appearances. T. Rex, David Bowie, Roxy Music, Alice Cooper, Suzi Quatro, Slade, the Sweet, Elton John, Queen and Sparks are among those featured in this compilation, which also sources footage from a selection of BBC concerts as well as shows including Crackerjack and Cilla

programme.title: Elton John: Electric Prom
programme.desc: The singer is joined by friend and mentor Leon Russell to perform songs from their collaborative album The Union, along with a selection of their most famous hits, at the Roundhouse in London. The duo are also joined on stage by guest singers Plan B and Rumer

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Emlyn, is a 13-year-old sheep farmer who lives in Wales. All year round, alongside his dog Patch, Emlyn is responsible for over a thousand sheep, helping them to deliver healthy baby lambs that will allow the family to survive. As a bitter winter hits the farm, hundreds of lambs' lives are at risk and Emlyn rushes to help them pull through. He's following in a long tradition - his family has been farming for more than 150 years - but tragedy struck when Emlyn was four years old when his father died. Now he takes responsibility for helping his mum to keep the farm going, which is crucial for the family's survival. Emlyn combines life as a secondary school student with the demands of a busy sheep farm. He's up at 6.30 every morning, touring the farm on his quadbike before breakfast, and then again for a few hours after school. There are always jobs to do from rescuing sheep that have escaped to spreading manure on the fields, fixing fences and feeding the sheep. As the year progresses Em

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Ashwika is passionate about protecting India’s only ape: the hoolock gibbon. Spending most of their lives in the canopy and rarely coming to ground means their populations are hugely threatened by deforestation, with their population decreasing by 90 per cent in just 30 years. Four years ago, Ashwika travelled to Assam and met a gibbon that touched her heart, and from that moment on she has wanted to protect them. On this mission she is going to a village where the people live peacefully alongside the gibbons and have done for many generations, and learns about why they care about them so much. Along the wa,y she witnesses habitat loss with her own ‘eye in the sky’. And she meets one of India’s top gibbon scientists, who is doing everything he can to save the species with the help of local villagers.

Both children love playing with their families - laughing, smiling, clapping, playing peek-a-boo and making noise. Bonnie gets excited when her dad suggests a day out to the beach! When they get there, Bonnie is concentrating on making sandcastles before splashing her feet in the water, laughing and loving the sea. Harry and his family go on a bug hunt at a nature reserve, but what will they find? While the family enjoys a relaxing picnic, Harry tries a banana for the very first time. Bonnie is happily crawling along the beach finding seashells before mum lays out a picnic with yummy hummus and pitta bread. programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Sebastiaan's making a South African bobotie, which is a local dish his family have shared with him. He starts by putting chopped spring onions, grated courgette, turmeric powder, ground coriander and torn red pepper into a casserole dish and cooking them. He then adds garlic puree, ginger puree, chutney and chopped tomatoes to cooked beef mince. Sebastiaan then carefully makes powder out of his special ingredient, cumin seeds, and adds the powder to the beef mixture. Sebastiaan mixes both the vegetables and the beef in an oven dish and flattens them before adding two bay leaves. He adds a topping of milk and egg and then places it in the oven. Sebastiaan has a wonderfully decorated table, and he has another traditional treat to go with his bobotie. Will his friends figure out what his special ingredient is?

programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. This one is all about a seabird called a sooty tern. Jen has deployed one of her remote-controlled bird-cams, which is painted to look like a sooty tern, to a small island in the Indian Ocean. The live feed from the bird-cam will be played during Mr Hammonds talk. Jen gets the feed up on the screen to show Andy. Freya and Teddy must complete a number of challenges across the garden if they’re to make it. Harry and Henry the Hedgehogs and Jago the Robin are on hand to commentate. It isn’t easy; will they succeed?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Phinehas and Charlie as they both wait for their newborn baby brother or sister to arrive into the world. Charlie’s mum packs a bag ready to go to hospital with dad and waves goodbye to Charlie and Grandma, whilst Phinehas’ family gets ready to pop a gender reveal balloon to find out if the next arrival will be a boy or a girl. Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. After a long wait, it’s finally time for Charlie and Phinehas to meet their new siblings. Both babies travel home to their families and meet their big brothers for the very first time. Once Phinehas meets the new baby, he joins in with dad to register the birth of their new baby. Charlie meets a health visitor, who has come to visit to check on the baby and make sure it is staying healthy and strong.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Holly comes from the English city, Liverpool. She will be making two tasty recipes. The first is a local Merseyside stew called scouse, made with chopped and cooked carrots, potatoes and onions with gravy, cooked beef, bay leaves and her special ingredient: celery. The second is a side of crusty bread. Holly makes a dough and stirs in yoghurt and honey. Then it is topped with more yoghurt and pumpkin seeds and placed in the oven. Holly also serves pickled beetroot and cabbage, a traditional side to scouse. Will Holly's friends enjoy her delicious food, and will they enjoy the celery once it is cooked in her recipe?

Andy has to clear all the leaves off the path in the Amazon Dome, which is a huge task, but luckily Jen has invented a new machine to help him. It’s a superpowered leaf blower! Heading to Jungle Den to find it, Andy bumps into Mr Hammond, who is unpacking from his photography expedition to the Amazon River. He managed to photograph a particularly rare fish, which has not been named yet but belongs to a group of fish called cichlids. Mr Hammond has arranged a press conference to reveal his photo but can’t work out how to get the image off the memory card. programme.desc: Join Stevie and Conor as they begin to discover the world of sound and see how their hearing is developed. Conor’s big brother Harrison is here to help show him around all his different musical toys, including a noisy rattle. In the garden, Stevie hangs out with mum and dad and listens to all the different sounds before getting ready to go out. Meanwhile, Conor heads over to grandma’s house while listening for sounds with his mum and brother. Stevie gets a quick hearing test at the hospital before heading home to make lots of noise with household objects. programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Leo's family is from Russia, and they have shown him how to make a special sharlotka, a simple Russian apple cake. Leo starts by separating the eggs. He then adds sugar and flour to the yolks and mixes them together until the mixture is smooth. He whisks the egg whites until they are stiff and stirs them into the yolk mix. After this, Leo cuts apples into small chunks and places half of them in a cake tin, pours over the egg mixture and puts the rest of the apples on top. Then the sharlotka is ready to be baked in the oven. Leo even has an extra special Russian-themed surprise for his friends while they eat! Can they work out what his special ingredient is?
