programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating mark the start of the first lockdown with an hour-long special looking at some of the more positive stories and achievements of the past 12 months, and celebrating how people across the country have stepped up to help others

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Metal expert Dominic Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk join forces on a wooden travelling trunk, which was used by a family of Polish refugees as they travelled across continents looking for a new home after the Second World War. Steve Fletcher has trouble getting a wobbly toy cyclist back on the straight and narrow, Brenton West tries to make an old bell ring again and Kirsten Ramsay is asked to repair a damaged glass trophy, which a couple want to pass on in memory of their late daughter, the original recipient

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts this property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house. It's then up to celebrity judges Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix - joined each week by a special guest - to watch the four 'owners' interact and figure out which one is telling the truth and who are the impostors. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen joins in the fun for the first edition, set in a converted barn in Ashford, Kent

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Belgium v Wales. Jason Mohammad presents action from both teams' opening 2022 FIFA World Cup Group E qualifier at Den Dreef in Leuven, as Wales took on the team at the top of the world rankings

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The documentary moves to Newcastle with the stories of survivors who suffered at the hands of George Ormond, a youth coach connected to Newcastle United - revealing how one former player tried to secretly record a confession from his abuser. Eventually, the three separate cases are brought to trial, but the question remains - why did it take so long to reach this point?

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Caitlin is now pregnant with her boss Kate's baby, and they navigate the complex relationship between a surrogate and intended parent, while also working together every day. Gay couple Alex and Richard begin the awkward process of home inseminations with their surrogate, Maddie, while single man David and surrogate Lauren end their relationship

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on a three-and-a-half year project whose auction lot included a fish and chip shop, a flat and a large storage area in Fareham in Hampshire. Dion Dublin checks out a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands, while Lucy Alexander takes a look at a studio flat in London's South Norwood. Two of the three projects prove really challenging, with some unforeseen problems to overcome

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley are live, asking for viewers' help with unsolved cases around the country

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In a Lanarkshire forest, a quad biking accident tests the ambulance service's specialist off-road vehicle to its limits, as Paramedic John struggles to reach the injured patient. In Glasgow, John and Suzanne rush to the scene of a nasty bicycle accident, and in the Borders Lee and Nadine try and ease the pain of a patient with a suspected dislocated shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear meets the infamous Miranda Evans and Sid puzzles over his first labour. Emma has to get a fractured family to come together when a graduation day becomes too much

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Shropshire countryside to help a couple with a £335,000 budget find a rural family home closer to loved ones. Exploring more of the county's bricks and mortar, Jonnie also visits the recently restored 18th-century walled garden at Apley Estate, where he samples freshly grown culinary delights

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Father-and-son Mark and Henry are passionate about good pork, having a smallholding where they rear a heritage pig breed in the Wirral. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as the pair bring some some friendly rivalry to the kitchen, aided by chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher and woodwork expert Will Kirk cannot resist staging a competitive game of pinball on a penny slot machine in need of rejuvenation. Other items seeking attention are a 1960s hand-painted drag racing cone and a pair of art deco-style silver ladies holding mirrors. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray gets an unexpected call from school regarding an issue with one of the children, and when Whitney offers support, he uses the opportunity to plant more seeds of doubt about Kush. Ruby tells Jean she will ring the police and drop the charges against Stacey, but soon she has more to worry about after bad news from the doctor. Ben helps Kheerat teach the thief a lesson

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: An epic journey following Wales's rivers, from their birth in the mountains to the sea, discovering the hidden secrets of wildlife. Super slow-motion cameras film peregrine falcons on the hunt and reveal why their prey, the unsuspecting red grouse, are often able to evade the fastest predator on Earth. Dragonflies' amazing aerial acrobatics are slowed down 400 times their normal speed and red squirrels are captured as they leap through the trees. In a city park, pigeons hidden superpowers are laid bare, and in an upland garden a subtle hierarchy is at play as birds gather around a feeder

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat gives Vinny an opportunity to prove himself by looking after Stas's associate - but he is disheartened when he learns he is to be his driver. Kush plans a romantic evening but Whitney has to work at Gray's, so he brings the date night to her. But when Gray returns home, he is furious to see what is happening

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last heat sees the final five amateurs cook their Signature Dish - something that tells judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace about their potential, as they pick their two favourites with those cooks earning a place in the next round. The remaining three have to pull out all the stops as they cook-off in a new test - the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. After which, those remaining must cook two courses that excite not just John and Gregg but also to former champions Thomasina Miers and Tim Anderson, as they vie for a quarter-final place and the chance to be MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last quarter-final sees the week's quartet of talented heat-winners return to fight for a semi-final place by facing the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by celebrated food critic Grace Dent - is to cook a dish inspired by global street food. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Medical journalist Michael Mosley and a team of scientists investigate some of the easiest and most effective ways to get the biggest benefits of exercise, and turning what we think we know about fitness on its head. The team reveal why walking 10,000 steps a day is just a marketing ploy, and that two minutes of intense exercise is all you need per week. Elsewhere in the programme, they also discuss ways to help you stick to your fitness plan, and discuss exercises that can actually have a positive impact on brain activity

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Wimbledon to meet a pair of young developers with big plans for a two-bed house. He also visits a three-bed semi in Birmingham. Dion Dublin is in Bootle, Merseyside, to cast his eye over a two-bed semi-detached house

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases around the country

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics respond to a major incident on the A90 near Dundee. A crash between a van and a bus has led to multiple casualties, including one patient thrown from the top deck of the bus. Team Leader Mark must prioritise who needs the most urgent care as he works with dozens of other emergency responders at the scene of the crash

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction. Natasha also finds out about the value of provenance

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Here, artists travel to the Wirral to create artworks to complement a recently completed house renovation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple on their country house hunt in Wiltshire. They have a generous £1million budget to afford them space for their prized possessions - a baby grand piano and classic cars. Away from the property search, Nicki discovers more about the art of wooden hat block making, a critically endangered heritage craft being kept alive by a Wiltshire-based family whose blocks are used by milliners across the world

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts, as London-based sisters Kavita and Sangeeta compete. The siblings both have busy lives and would love to get some new ideas for quick tasty meals they can make when they get home after a long day. They are aided in the kitchen by chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Art conservator Lucia Scalisi revives an oil painting of a mining scene, created by an amateur artist in the 1960s. Embroidery expert Sara Dennis and silversmith Brenton West join forces on a delicate wedding tiara seeking attention, while Steve Fletcher takes receipt of an early 20th-century chiming mantlepiece clock that was gifted to a couple on their wedding day. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particula struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian has never been very good at DIY, finding even the simple task of hanging a picture invariably ending in disaster. So it remains to be seen how he will cope after turning up at one of Wales's biggest building sites and being expected to get stuck in. From bricklaying to plumbing and carpentry, Rhod has to go all out to prove he has what it takes to become a master builder

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Liam Neeson and Micheal Richardson, who are playing father and son in comedy-drama Made in Italy. Breakout star of The Serpent, Tahar Rahim, talks about his gripping new legal drama The Mauritanian, while Cush Jumbo reflects on five-part thriller The Beast Must Die, in which he stars. Comedian and actor Rob Brydon and the undisputed queen of Dragons' Den Deborah Meaden also feature. With music by singer-songwriter Laura Mvula

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.title: Citizen Khan
programme.desc: Mr Khan is made to sleep in the car after forgetting his wedding anniversary, and his mission to make amends leads him into further trouble at Edgbaston cricket ground. His boast to Dave that he knows local boy Moeen Ali backfires when Amjad suggests he could get the famous England all-rounder to make a celebrity appearance at the fundraiser that Mrs Khan and Shazia are helping organise. Guest starring Mayor of London Sadiq Khan, Test Match Special's Jonathan Agnew and former England cricket captain Michael Vaughan

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Festive instalment of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Tom Allen is the host for the first in a new run of stand-up showcases, performed in front of a live audience at London's world-famous Hammersmith Apollo. Each show features sets from two of the country's most acclaimed up-and-coming comedians, and this week it is Rosie Jones and Kae Kurd taking to the stage. Performers set to appear in this series include Sindhu Vee, Alex Edelman, Lou Sanders, Darren Harriott, Jessica Fostekew, Stephen Bailey, Desiree Burch, Paul McCaffrey, Jonny Pelham, Ardal O'Hanlon, Sara Barron, Mawaan Rizwan, Guz Khan, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Rhys James, Sarah Pascoe, Chris McCausland and Flo & Joan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Sally Nugent and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in the Devon town of Seaton, Crewe in Cheshire and London's South Norwood

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley offer expert advice at Southwell racecourse, Nottinghamshire, as two good friends take on a father and son-in-law. Anita Manning hosts

programme.title: First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Kirsten Campbell presents live coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid receives a challenge from an old friend and Valerie gets a mysterious letter. Luca deals with a family whose need for travel vaccinations leads to trouble

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits the Peak District with a couple returning from the Netherlands, who want their generous £1.5million budget to provide accommodation for their visiting mothers

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Electrics expert Mark Stuckey and silversmith Brenton West join forces to repair a much-loved, sparkling chandelier that once took pride of place in a council house. Furniture restorer Will Kirk is presented with a damaged mother of pearl table, badly burned after a hot pan was mistakenly placed on its surface. And mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a broken toy aeroplane that doubles up as a memento of a lost childhood in Iraq

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating mark the start of the first lockdown with an hour-long special looking at some of the more positive stories and achievements of the past 12 months, and celebrating how people across the country have stepped up to help others

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton's final loch-hopping journey begins in the forgotten lands of Morvern. On the shores of Loch Aline, he sees the devil in a fossil, then plunges deep underground to discover an unlikely connection between sand and the Second World War. At Fort William he paces the ruins of the original fort and learns how to make Jacobite bank notes, before ending his journey at a 'loch that never was' beneath the 300ft Steall Falls

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Fed up of playing second fiddle to Kim, Rebecca resorts to desperate measures to get Angus's attention, while Amber is determined to step up as Rory's life hangs in the balance. With his debts mounting, Stevie contemplates ways to make some quick cash

programme.title: This Is My House

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer invites contributors including Joel Sked, Shaughan McGuigan, Craig Fowler and Robert Borthwick to dissect the ins and outs of the Scottish footballing week just passed, offering analysis and opinion in their own unique way. As well as the current season, the panel also delves into footballing history to make a case to enshrine a moment of Scottish football history, for future generations to feast upon

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Pete and Gary are called to a patient in Paisley who has fallen down the stairs, and quickly realise he needs more support than just medical care. Martyn and Kevin treat an elderly patient who has fallen on an icy pedestrianised street

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley offer expert advice at Southwell racecourse, Nottinghamshire, as two good friends take on a father and son-in-law. Anita Manning hosts

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid receives a challenge from an old friend and Valerie gets a mysterious letter. Luca deals with a family whose need for travel vaccinations leads to trouble

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits the Peak District with a couple returning from the Netherlands, who want their generous £1.5million budget to provide accommodation for their visiting mothers

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband and wife Raymond and Karen are both vegan but are bored of making the same old thing. Paired with chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, they hope to come away with some new dishes for their repertoire. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Electrics expert Mark Stuckey and silversmith Brenton West join forces to repair a much-loved, sparkling chandelier that once took pride of place in a council house. Furniture restorer Will Kirk is presented with a damaged mother of pearl table, badly burned after a hot pan was mistakenly placed on its surface. And mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a broken toy aeroplane that doubles up as a memento of a lost childhood in Iraq

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating mark the start of the first lockdown with an hour-long special looking at some of the more positive stories and achievements of the past 12 months, and celebrating how people across the country have stepped up to help others

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Metal expert Dominic Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk join forces on a wooden travelling trunk, which was used by a family of Polish refugees as they travelled across continents looking for a new home after the Second World War. Steve Fletcher has trouble getting a wobbly toy cyclist back on the straight and narrow, Brenton West tries to make an old bell ring again and Kirsten Ramsay is asked to repair a damaged glass trophy, which a couple want to pass on in memory of their late daughter, the original recipient

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts this property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house. It's then up to celebrity judges Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix - joined each week by a special guest - to watch the four 'owners' interact and figure out which one is telling the truth and who are the impostors. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen joins in the fun for the first edition, set in a converted barn in Ashford, Kent

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: In 1972 Mary Peters won the Olympic gold medal in the women's pentathlon in the Munich Games, becoming a hero in Northern Ireland and beyond. She reveals to Stephen Watson the story behind her win, how she defied threats and why her last medal win in 1974 was particularly poignant

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The documentary moves to Newcastle with the stories of survivors who suffered at the hands of George Ormond, a youth coach connected to Newcastle United - revealing how one former player tried to secretly record a confession from his abuser. Eventually, the three separate cases are brought to trial, but the question remains - why did it take so long to reach this point?

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Caitlin is now pregnant with her boss Kate's baby, and they navigate the complex relationship between a surrogate and intended parent, while also working together every day. Gay couple Alex and Richard begin the awkward process of home inseminations with their surrogate, Maddie, while single man David and surrogate Lauren end their relationship

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on a three-and-a-half year project whose auction lot included a fish and chip shop, a flat and a large storage area in Fareham in Hampshire. Dion Dublin checks out a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands, while Lucy Alexander takes a look at a studio flat in London's South Norwood. Two of the three projects prove really challenging, with some unforeseen problems to overcome

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley are live, asking for viewers' help with unsolved cases around the country

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In a Lanarkshire forest, a quad biking accident tests the ambulance service's specialist off-road vehicle to its limits, as Paramedic John struggles to reach the injured patient. In Glasgow, John and Suzanne rush to the scene of a nasty bicycle accident, and in the Borders Lee and Nadine try and ease the pain of a patient with a suspected dislocated shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear meets the infamous Miranda Evans and Sid puzzles over his first labour. Emma has to get a fractured family to come together when a graduation day becomes too much

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Shropshire countryside to help a couple with a £335,000 budget find a rural family home closer to loved ones. Exploring more of the county's bricks and mortar, Jonnie also visits the recently restored 18th-century walled garden at Apley Estate, where he samples freshly grown culinary delights

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Father-and-son Mark and Henry are passionate about good pork, having a smallholding where they rear a heritage pig breed in the Wirral. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as the pair bring some some friendly rivalry to the kitchen, aided by chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher and woodwork expert Will Kirk cannot resist staging a competitive game of pinball on a penny slot machine in need of rejuvenation. Other items seeking attention are a 1960s hand-painted drag racing cone and a pair of art deco-style silver ladies holding mirrors. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray gets an unexpected call from school regarding an issue with one of the children, and when Whitney offers support, he uses the opportunity to plant more seeds of doubt about Kush. Ruby tells Jean she will ring the police and drop the charges against Stacey, but soon she has more to worry about after bad news from the doctor. Ben helps Kheerat teach the thief a lesson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat gives Vinny an opportunity to prove himself by looking after Stas's associate - but he is disheartened when he learns he is to be his driver. Kush plans a romantic evening but Whitney has to work at Gray's, so he brings the date night to her. But when Gray returns home, he is furious to see what is happening

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last heat sees the final five amateurs cook their Signature Dish - something that tells judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace about their potential, as they pick their two favourites with those cooks earning a place in the next round. The remaining three have to pull out all the stops as they cook-off in a new test - the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. After which, those remaining must cook two courses that excite not just John and Gregg but also to former champions Thomasina Miers and Tim Anderson, as they vie for a quarter-final place and the chance to be MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last quarter-final sees the week's quartet of talented heat-winners return to fight for a semi-final place by facing the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by celebrated food critic Grace Dent - is to cook a dish inspired by global street food. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers follows the ups and downs of the political parties at Stormont and Westminster and debates the highs and lows of the political week. Plus, an alternative view on the week's political headlines

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Medical journalist Michael Mosley and a team of scientists investigate some of the easiest and most effective ways to get the biggest benefits of exercise, and turning what we think we know about fitness on its head. The team reveal why walking 10,000 steps a day is just a marketing ploy, and that two minutes of intense exercise is all you need per week. Elsewhere in the programme, they also discuss ways to help you stick to your fitness plan, and discuss exercises that can actually have a positive impact on brain activity

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Wimbledon to meet a pair of young developers with big plans for a two-bed house. He also visits a three-bed semi in Birmingham. Dion Dublin is in Bootle, Merseyside, to cast his eye over a two-bed semi-detached house

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases around the country

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics respond to a major incident on the A90 near Dundee. A crash between a van and a bus has led to multiple casualties, including one patient thrown from the top deck of the bus. Team Leader Mark must prioritise who needs the most urgent care as he works with dozens of other emergency responders at the scene of the crash

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction. Natasha also finds out about the value of provenance

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Here, artists travel to the Wirral to create artworks to complement a recently completed house renovation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple on their country house hunt in Wiltshire. They have a generous £1million budget to afford them space for their prized possessions - a baby grand piano and classic cars. Away from the property search, Nicki discovers more about the art of wooden hat block making, a critically endangered heritage craft being kept alive by a Wiltshire-based family whose blocks are used by milliners across the world

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts, as London-based sisters Kavita and Sangeeta compete. The siblings both have busy lives and would love to get some new ideas for quick tasty meals they can make when they get home after a long day. They are aided in the kitchen by chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Art conservator Lucia Scalisi revives an oil painting of a mining scene, created by an amateur artist in the 1960s. Embroidery expert Sara Dennis and silversmith Brenton West join forces on a delicate wedding tiara seeking attention, while Steve Fletcher takes receipt of an early 20th-century chiming mantlepiece clock that was gifted to a couple on their wedding day. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Guests Joanne McNally and Jimeoin join regulars Neil Delamere and Colin Murphy as they raise a socially distanced laugh or two in response to current events. Hosted by Tim McGarry

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Liam Neeson and Micheal Richardson, who are playing father and son in comedy-drama Made in Italy. Breakout star of The Serpent, Tahar Rahim, talks about his gripping new legal drama The Mauritanian, while Cush Jumbo reflects on five-part thriller The Beast Must Die, in which he stars. Comedian and actor Rob Brydon and the undisputed queen of Dragons' Den Deborah Meaden also feature. With music by singer-songwriter Laura Mvula

programme.title: La La Land (2016)
programme.desc: Jobbing actress Mia and struggling jazz pianist Sebastian fall in love and attempt to realise their respective dreams in Los Angeles - a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. However, keeping the wolf from the door without compromising their beliefs proves harder than expected. Damien Chazelle's Bafta-winning musical, starring Emma Stone, Ryan Gosling and John Legend

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Joint Committee on the National Security Strategy session on the machinery of national security with evidence from former Prime Minister Theresa May and former Chancellor and Defence Secretary Lord Hammond of Runnymede, from Monday 22 March

programme.title: The Murder of Mr Perceval
programme.desc: Simon Vaughan looks at the assassination of serving Prime Minister Spencer Perceval in the lobby of the House of Commons in 1812

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Questions to UK government ministers in the House of Lords, from Tuesday 23 March

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: UK government news conference on the Covid-19 response in England, with prime minister Boris Johnson, chief medical officer for England professor Chris Whitty, and UK government chief scientific adviser Sir Patrick Vallance, from Tuesday 23 March

programme.title: Tuesday in Parliament
programme.desc: Presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.desc: When Bing, Sula and Flop look for stones, Sula finds an ammonite fossil

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo

programme.title: Gaisgich Oga an Darna Cogaidh
programme.desc: Calum does not care about the war and refuses to practise boring air raid drills. But while he is on his bike, German bombers fly over the town of Clydebank

programme.title: Air a' Chanal (Scotland's Canals)
programme.desc: Niall Iain MacDonald and Calum Macaulay explore the Crinan Canal. At nine miles long, it is the shortest of the Scottish Canals, cutting through stunning Argyll

programme.title: Mach à Seo!

programme.title: Sruth
programme.desc: Music from Lauren O'Neill, Ali Levack, Griogair Labhruidh and Niall Vallely, along with Kim Carnie, Martin Meehan, Hamish Napier and Lucy Kerr

programme.title: Na Trads
programme.desc: Robert Robertson presents music from the Trad Awards archives, featuring Treacherous Orchestra, Duncan Chisholm and Man's Ruin

programme.desc: The mudskipper tells how he is a special fish as he can live in and out of water - a bit like a frog or a toad

programme.desc: Maggie starts a new business delivering boxes of vegetables straight from the farm

programme.desc: The joy of spring is everywhere and Ronia and Biorc catch two wild horses

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda wakes up with a big pimple on her face, and decides not to go out to avoid being mocked by people

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain
programme.desc: Neen Mackay visits the annual Salmon Festival in Inverness. She meets with female anglers who are fast becoming a major part of the world of fly fishing, and with passionate competitors in casting. With the salmon species now under serious threat, the programme also considers some of the scientific debates currently going on within the industry

programme.title: Iron Women/Boireannaich Iarainn
programme.desc: The history of women's golf in Scotland. From the early pioneers of the 18th century to formidable role models who challenged the patriarchal constraints of male-dominated golfing arenas, this story celebrates the trailblazers who put Scottish women's golf firmly on the world map

programme.title: Iùl a' Chiùil (The Musical Path)

programme.desc: Cameras follow Orinoco the osprey as he builds his strength up for his first epic migration. Starting in Canada, Orinoco faces treacherous weather, getting lost and starvation on his journey to South America, relying on his fishing technique and built-in navigational abilities to get him to the fish-filled waters of Colombia

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Crùbag is preparing fondue today

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Seumas from Harris talks about his favourite hobbies, including piping and fishing

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley

programme.desc: Donald Morrison introduces vintage news reports from the Highlands and islands, including a portrayal of the activities of the Barra lifeboat and highlighting how work on the Tràigh Mhòr disrupted planes landing on Barra, and how the Eriskay Co-operative was reviving that island

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar travels to Point in Lewis, where she meets Maggie Mackenzie, known to many as the actress who played Fiona Macaskill in the long-running soap Machair

programme.title: About Britain: Isle of Skye
programme.desc: In 1954, Richard Dimbleby and a BBC film crew visited the Isle of Skye to record an episode of the early British television series About Britain. Here, he offers those viewing across the United Kingdom an insight into life on the island, meeting local characters and gaining an understanding of the lifestyle and culture of islanders

programme.title: Port

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Mo, Alison, Rebecca and Gary pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mack leaves Aaron in trouble. Priya awkwardly avoids Ellis. Nicola tries to reassure Jimmy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne pays the price for passing herself off as a nurse, while Toyah thinks she is having an affair. Alina tears a strip off Tyrone for telling Fiz about what happened, and Jenny tells Brian he's barred for making nasty comments online about Steve

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. The discovery of a new variant of Covid-19 causes chaos in Heathrow as airports around the world begin to close their borders to the UK. As flights are cancelled before the passengers' eyes, they scramble to get on any available plane in a desperate attempt to get home

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natasha admits to Nick that she still has feelings for him, while he thinks Toyah is right, and believes Leanne is having an affair. Cathy begs Steve to put an end to the vendetta being waged against them

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, father and son duo Martin and Roman Kemp delve into their family history which spans North London and the North East. As Roman's journey takes him to the British Museum, Martin comes face to face with a past that was never spoken about as he hears tales of female heroism. Later, the pair are shocked to find ancestors leading parallel lives with their own

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v San Marino (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' opening Group I 2022 FIFA World Cup qualifier, held at Wembley Stadium. The hosts have met San Marino on eight previous occasions and have recorded an aggregate score of 37-1 over those matches, meaning they will be expecting nothing less than a convincing victory this evening. The most recent encounter took place in September 2015, when England won 6-0, with Theo Walcott bagging a brace. With analysis from Eni Aluko, Roy Keane and Ian Wright, interviews by Gabriel Clarke and commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five, The Bright Sparks, answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Vera, Lee, Joyce and Ben pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v San Marino (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' opening Group I 2022 FIFA World Cup qualifier, held at Wembley Stadium. The hosts have met San Marino on eight previous occasions and have recorded an aggregate score of 37-1 over those matches, meaning they will be expecting nothing less than a convincing victory this evening. The most recent encounter took place in September 2015, when England won 6-0, with Theo Walcott bagging a brace. With analysis from Eni Aluko, Roy Keane and Ian Wright, interviews by Gabriel Clarke and commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from the opening 2022 FIFA World Cup qualifiers, including England v San Marino at Wembley Stadium, Scotland v Austria at Hampden Park, Italy v Northern Ireland at Stadio Ennio Tardini and Belgium v Wales at Den Dreef. With analysis from Lee Dixon, Ian Wright and Ally McCoist

programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.desc: A grandmother is suspected of snatching a baby from outside a shop following a family quarrel, but Kate believes one of her patients may be responsible. programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Mum is wooed by a middle-aged Lothario who not only tries to take over the entire house, but even manages to con an unwitting Stan and Arthur into doing his laundry

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A radio control system is introduced at the depot, but Jack and Stan bring chaos to the airwaves, treating the new technology as nothing more than a lark

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon is eager to halt a crimewave before the Assistant Chief Constable arrives for a shoot on Lord Ashfordly's estate, while Kate is faced with a medical and moral dilemma. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is snappy with her staff, and later arranges a date with Denise. Betty suggests Turner takes Bernice back on. Roy confides his feelings about Kelly to Biff

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy asks for Betty's advice about Bernice. The vet's surgery is under pressure when Zoe does not turn up for work. Bernice makes an immediate impact at the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy enjoys being the centre of controversy. Zoe pays Toyah to babysit so she can go out with Ashley. Ken presses Deirdre for details over her split with Jon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre considers moving to London to be closer to Tracy. Ken begs Deirdre to trust him as he fears Jon is threatening her

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Syndicate 9 find themselves dealing with a high-profile historical case after Mandy Sweeting's body is recovered from a quarry, 23 years after she was first reported missing. However, Rachel's biggest challenge might not be dealing with the media attention or even re-interviewing bereft family members, but hiding her contempt for Rob Waddington's father Frankie (former Coronation Street star Ian Mercer), who led the original investigation. Meanwhile, Janet also has problems to deal with at home, as Elise moves in with her dad and his new girlfriend. But at least it inspires the detective to look at her own love life, as she confides in Rachel that she's started using dating sites. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al tries to raise some quick cash, and offers to sort out all Louisa's household and plumbing problems if she can pay him promptly. The teacher gets good news on the employment front, and Martin accidentally inflicts a minor wound on an elderly patient - who responds by walking out of the surgery and vowing never to return. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Phyllida Law and Benjamin Whitrow

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider and Roy spend the night in a tent outside Alf's house. Zoe tells Ashley she is desperate for a night out. Ken is shocked when Deirdre tells him the wedding is off

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn gets fed up with Ned's pride and wonders whether he will go to Ibiza with her. Mandy is upset when Tricia spills a drink on her CV. Ned's bear goes to auction

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alan is not amused when Terry asks about the bar job. Rachel offers to go out with Zoe for moral support. Mandy and Tricia's rivalry finally boils over

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A grandmother is suspected of snatching a baby from outside a shop following a family quarrel, but Kate believes one of her patients may be responsible. Meanwhile, Greengrass buys a racehorse and begins to train it on the streets of Aidensfield - much to the dismay of Sergeant Blaketon. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is delighted when Mike announces his intention to marry into wealth - until a close link between the two families is uncovered

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Mum is wooed by a middle-aged Lothario who not only tries to take over the entire house, but even manages to con an unwitting Stan and Arthur into doing his laundry

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A radio control system is introduced at the depot, but Jack and Stan bring chaos to the airwaves, treating the new technology as nothing more than a lark

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon is eager to halt a crimewave before the Assistant Chief Constable arrives for a shoot on Lord Ashfordly's estate, while Kate is faced with a medical and moral dilemma. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is snappy with her staff, and later arranges a date with Denise. Betty suggests Turner takes Bernice back on. Roy confides his feelings about Kelly to Biff

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy asks for Betty's advice about Bernice. The vet's surgery is under pressure when Zoe does not turn up for work. Bernice makes an immediate impact at the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy enjoys being the centre of controversy. Zoe pays Toyah to babysit so she can go out with Ashley. Ken presses Deirdre for details over her split with Jon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre considers moving to London to be closer to Tracy. Ken begs Deirdre to trust him as he fears Jon is threatening her

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Syndicate 9 find themselves dealing with a high-profile historical case after Mandy Sweeting's body is recovered from a quarry, 23 years after she was first reported missing. However, Rachel's biggest challenge might not be dealing with the media attention or even re-interviewing bereft family members, but hiding her contempt for Rob Waddington's father Frankie (former Coronation Street star Ian Mercer), who led the original investigation. Meanwhile, Janet also has problems to deal with at home, as Elise moves in with her dad and his new girlfriend. But at least it inspires the detective to look at her own love life, as she confides in Rachel that she's started using dating sites. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan begins courting Blakey's demure, well-to-do niece, who tries to smarten him up - but it only takes one meeting with his family to make her realise he is a hopeless case. Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Workers at a visiting funfair get their hands on a stolen painting and sell it to Greengrass. In characteristic fashion he sets out to make a quick return on his investment and wastes no time in trying to offload it on Dr Ferrenby - who realises its value and informs Nick. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel urges Kathy to find out how Chris really feels. Paddy is annoyed that Zoe will not get out of bed. Bernice instructs Tricia to stop flirting so much

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lyn fails her mock exams and is sacked by Zoe. Kathy seriously considers getting back together with Chris. Paddy tries to smooth things over with Jack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe is annoyed when Ashley gets at her for abandoning Shannon. Deirdre receives a shock when she visits the bank manager. Les gets a job as a security guard

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police search Deirdre's house for clues, and Frankie warns Mike that Ms Rachid could go to prison. Maud assures Ashley that Zoe will be fine in the hostel

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel promised the chaos of her personal life was all in the past, but she is conflicted upon discovering her mother's new boyfriend is a violent domestic abuser. Meanwhile, Janet gets cold feet when she goes on her first date, terrified at the idea of having to impress a stranger. The crimebusters are united in the search for the killer of Rich Hutchings, who is found murdered in the flat he shares with his husband Adam. Barry, their outspoken and violent homophobic neighbour, is the prime suspect. However, as Adam's friend Tim struggles to recall the events of their night out, it seems Barry may be in the clear. Meanwhile, Rachel is horrified when her mum Sharon turns up asking for help. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

programme.desc: One of the firm's clippies gets evicted leaving Stan feeling guilty as he was late getting her home, so he persuades Mum to take her in as a lodger. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates a break-in at the surgery, while Kate discovers the cause of a patient's miscarriages and Greengrass claims his car has been stolen. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy confronts Zoe about the Sugden fiasco. Bernice suggests to Mandy that she dresses more conservatively. Zoe tells Chris he may still have a chance with Kathy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laura tries to cheer Chris up at work. Stella moves into Home Farm and Eric offers to be her estate manager. Jack and Sarah are disgusted by Zoe's attempt at bribery

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider organises the protestors on the Red Rec. Ken tries hard to cheer Deirdre up. Alec goes to Switzerland to visit Vicky. Des aims to prove his commitment to Samantha

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley rows with Fred about Zoe. The Malletts return and Judy feels she will go mad knowing Katie is just across the street. Deirdre is formally charged by the police

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is delighted by the arrival of his older brother Sam, but their reunion turns to misery when Martin delivers bad news to one of them. Louisa searches for a new home in the village and visits Edith for a check-up, where she reveals an embarrassing piece of information, and Al makes his situation with Pauline worse by trying to act on advice from Bert. Starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al tries to raise some quick cash, and offers to sort out all Louisa's household and plumbing problems if she can pay him promptly. The teacher gets good news on the employment front, and Martin accidentally inflicts a minor wound on an elderly patient - who responds by walking out of the surgery and vowing never to return. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Phyllida Law and Benjamin Whitrow

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar

programme.desc: A look back at memorable stagings of the race from Epsom Downs. Featuring brilliant performances from Shergar to Sea The Stars, plus interviews with personalities from the worlds of sport and entertainment

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is held hostage by former friend Frankie Connor, who is now a prisoner on the run and desperate to know whether his wife is having an affair with Italian restaurateur Luigi. Final episode of the light-hearted drama, starring George Cole, Gary Webster and Glynn Edwards

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A temperamental film actress becomes the apparent victim of an abduction from the set of her latest movie, and Simon must find out whether her disappearance is a publicity stunt or something altogether more sinister. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: With Haskins in Toronto, his stand-in DCI Quirk (guest star Bill Maynard) becomes obsessed with catching a gang of thieves. When information is received suggesting the crooks are about to rob a jeweller's shop, officers are poised for action, but events take an unexpected turn as the informant seemingly has a score to settle with Quirk first. Hard-hitting police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Two Russian spies escape from the prison Oldhill Monastery, with the help of some lizard vodka, and eliminate their targets. The third is about to escape - and his plan is to assassinate Steed. Patrick Macnee stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur attends an Italian wedding as guest of honour, and when Terry is held hostage at a launderette, the conman decides to sell the story to the media. First episode of the comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Featuring a family farm making jam for the Queen's breakfast table, a Sheffield man producing classic safety razors, a bed-maker using recycled bottles to make mattresses, and a British clockmaker paying tribute to a man that changed the course of history. Ricky Tomlinson narrates

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the afternoon session on day three at Celtic Manor resort in Newport, Wales, featuring the first eight frames of the third quarter-final. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Germany to investigate tales of a beast that has been on the prowl in European waters for the past 700 years. His investigations take him to Spain, where he discovers first-hand that as a consequence of a science experiment gone wrong, the Wels catfish is growing to man-eating proportions and is capable of everything depicted in medieval records

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: A look at the underwater predators the crews and skippers encounter, revealing how they trap and destroy their prey, and play havoc with catch rates

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A profile of a family making traditional haggis, revealing the dish may not have come from Scotland after all. Plus, a look at what goes into making a set of bowls fit for a champion, how a firm makes the wheelbarrows that helped rebuild Britain, and a team making classic brogues in demand worldwide

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood takes a look back at some of the great footballing upsets, including Wimbledon's famous FA Cup final victory over Liverpool.

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the evening session on day three at Celtic Manor resort in Newport, Wales, featuring the conclusion of the third quarter-final. Three-time world champion Mark Selby saw off the challenge of current Masters title-holder Yan Bingtao at this stage last year in Milton Keynes, prevailing 9-6, and having booked his place this time around, 'the Jester from Leicester' will expect to be a force once again. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Hulk (2003)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Bruce Banner turns into the rampaging green Hulk after exposure to a huge dose of gamma radiation, prompting the military to embark on a no-holds-barred mission to destroy the monster. Meanwhile, Banner's unstable father resurfaces, holding the secret to his transformation. Comic-book adventure from director Ang Lee, starring Eric Bana, Jennifer Connelly, Sam Elliott and Nick Nolte

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Germany to investigate tales of a beast that has been on the prowl in European waters for the past 700 years. programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the evening session on day three at Celtic Manor resort in Newport, Wales, featuring the conclusion of the third quarter-final. programme.title: Drawers Off

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Maitre d' Fred and his team of matchmakers invite more singletons to the luxury hotel in the hope of finding love in the sun. A string of unhappy relationships has left 24-year-old Thalia with a phobia of kissing. Her date with 25-year-old Essex lad Jack date gets off to a great start after a discovery of a shared love of sharks. Widower Tony is looking for someone to share his twilight years and is paired with the glamorous Lesley, a fellow East Ender. However, a surprising differing of political opinions threatens to derail their date

programme.title: Green Room (2015)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wrestling promoter Dale Harvey hosts the first dinner party in Derby, where he hopes to impress aspiring thespian Beth Dunmore, business support manager Jo-Anna, mother-of-three Ondine Shardlow, and triathlete David Morris with a wrestling-themed extravaganza featuring large men in tight lycra. However, a fractious royal debate dampens the mood

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business support manager Jo-Anna hosts the second dinner party in Derby, and hopes to wow her guests with a glitz and glam evening - complete with ball gowns and poetry. However, David is hoping for a more lively night - and his antics do not impress fellow contestant Dale

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

With access to some of his victims, and the police team that finally cracked the case once flaws in the original investigation emerged, this film's new analysis of the facts explains why it took years before Worboys's long and violent campaign of terror against women was finally brought to an end
